description:
Only efficacy number today is ~15ms/site on a SYNTHETIC 87-site fixture (wrd25_200sites); CHANGELOG honestly logs a 1.66x speedup (below the 2-4x target). No mutation score on real code is public.

Run mutagen against at least one small, real Elixir library and report the kill rate + a few representative survived mutants in the README. The cross-project efficacy study (vault report 2026-05-18: atlas 0.67, builder 0.70) already has data — surface a sanitized version publicly.

Acceptance: README cites at least one real-codebase kill rate with context.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED
Session: 33781f87-780a-4c97-b7ae-fe26d3cec2b1
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/33781f87-780a-4c97-b7ae-fe26d3cec2b1.jsonl
Commit: 99fad508412b85794324b7c8a996b98159b3fd72
Branch: mutagen-bqd.2/real-world-score (off epic/bqd)

Summary:
Added a new "## Efficacy on real codebases" section to README.md, slotted
immediately after the existing "## Why mutagen_ex (and how it differs from
muzak)" section (added by .1) and before "## Install". It composes with that
positioning section — the qualitative pitch there now has empirical backing —
and is orthogonal to the existing "## Performance" section (kill-rate/correctness
vs wall-clock speed).

Content surfaced (sanitized from the vault cross-project synthesis report,
2026-05-18, which is marked audience: external/sharable):
- Self-study: 160 sites, aggregate kill rate 0.75, gaps in 8/22 tickets;
  the A<B<C verifier-signal (0.68 / 0.75 / 0.88).
- Cross-project study table: stdlib lib 0.75, Phoenix/Ecto app 0.67,
  Phoenix LiveView admin app 0.70 — the 0.65-0.75 convergence band with
  the "expect <0.85 on Phoenix apps" calibration note.
- Two representative survivors: the 0.00-kill-rate config-diff module, and
  the verifier-flagged {:error, changeset} arm that survived a redo.
- Honest caveats: equivalent-mutant lower bound, coverage-gated silence on
  structural code, host-OTP-app-start adoption gap, HEEx out of scope.
- A one-sentence stakeholder pitch.

Sanitization applied: internal project names (atlas/builder/engage) generalized
to codebase-shape descriptions; internal ticket IDs (atlas-3x3.4, mutagen-wrd.*),
private repo file paths, vault report paths, absolute worktree paths, and the
internal filing/cleanup pass all dropped.

Files touched:
- README.md (+91 lines, docs only)

Tests added: none. This is a documentation-only ticket — no Advances: field,
no spec subject, no lib/ or test/ changes. Acceptance criterion ("README cites
at least one real-codebase kill rate with context") is satisfied: README now
cites four real-codebase kill rates with full context.

Stubs flipped: none (no verification stubs in scope).

Deferred must requirements: none.

Local sanity check: mix compile --warnings-as-errors -> exit 0. No code paths
changed, so the full mix test suite was not re-run for this docs-only diff;
the orchestrator owns Phase 2 verification.

title: Real-world mutation score in README
